seoTitle: Rorebai Products | Data Converters, RF & Analog ICs | LiTong Distributor
seoDescription: Explore Rorebai product portfolio including high-speed ADCs/DACs, RF transceivers, precision op-amps, and power management ICs. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Rorebai?
faqs.answer: Rorebai offers four main product categories: (1) High-Speed Data Converters - ADCs and DACs with sampling rates from MSPS to GSPS, featuring high resolution and low power consumption; (2) RF Transceivers - wideband RF PLLs, transmitters, and receivers for wireless communication systems; (3) Precision Operational Amplifiers - high-precision op-amps with low offset, low noise, and wide bandwidth; (4) Power Management ICs - voltage regulators, DC-DC converters, and power monitors for various applications. Each category includes multiple series optimized for specific performance requirements and operating environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review each product category to find the Rorebai component matching your application requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Rorebai data converter for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Rorebai data converter depends on your application requirements: (1) For communication systems - choose high-speed ADCs/DACs with sampling rates above 100MSPS and high dynamic range; (2) For industrial measurement - select high-resolution converters (16-bit or higher) with excellent linearity; (3) For portable applications - choose low-power converters with power-down modes; (4) For automotive - select products with wide temperature range and high reliability. Key parameters to consider include resolution, sampling rate, power consumption, interface type, and temperature range. Contact LiTong FAE for detailed selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match the data converter to your resolution, speed, and power requirements.

faqs.question: What are the key features of Rorebai RF transceivers?
faqs.answer: Rorebai RF transceivers feature: (1) Wide frequency coverage - supporting multiple bands for various wireless standards; (2) High integration - combining RF PLL, transmitter, and receiver in single chip; (3) Low phase noise - ensuring high-quality signal transmission and reception; (4) Fast settling time - enabling rapid frequency hopping applications; (5) Flexible interface - supporting various digital interfaces for easy system integration; (6) Low power consumption - optimized for portable and battery-powered applications. The CBMRF900 series is specifically designed for 4G/5G base stations and massive MIMO systems with excellent noise and linearity performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider Rorebai RF transceivers for cost-effective wireless communication solutions.

faqs.question: How do Rorebai precision op-amps compare to competitors?
faqs.answer: Rorebai precision op-amps offer competitive advantages: (1) Low offset voltage - achieving microvolt-level input offset for high-precision applications; (2) Low noise - excellent noise performance for sensor signal conditioning; (3) Wide bandwidth - supporting high-speed signal processing requirements; (4) Rail-to-rail operation - maximizing dynamic range for low-voltage applications; (5) Cost-effective pricing - 20-30% lower than established competitors while maintaining quality; (6) Local support - rapid technical support from Rorebai and LiTong engineering teams. Rorebai op-amps are pin-compatible with popular industry standards for easy substitution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Rorebai op-amps offer excellent price-performance ratio with local technical support.

faqs.question: What is the quality and reliability of Rorebai products?
faqs.answer: Rorebai maintains high quality and reliability standards: (1) Design quality - rigorous design verification and validation processes; (2) Manufacturing - partnership with world-renowned foundries and packaging facilities; (3) Testing - comprehensive testing including wafer sort, final test, and burn-in; (4) Quality certifications - national high-tech enterprise and technology-oriented SME certifications; (5) Traceability - full lot traceability for quality tracking; (6) Field proven - successful deployments in industrial, communications, and automotive applications. Rorebai's quality management system is ISO 9001 certified, ensuring products meet stringent requirements for various applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Rorebai products meet industry-standard quality and reliability requirements.

categories.name: High-Speed ADCs
categories.description: Rorebai high-speed ADCs provide high-resolution analog-to-digital conversion for demanding applications. The portfolio includes pipelined and SAR architectures with sampling rates from 10MSPS to over 1GSPS, resolutions from 12-bit to 16-bit, and excellent dynamic performance for communications, instrumentation, and medical applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Rorebai High-Speed ADCs
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right ADC for your high-speed data acquisition application

faqs.question: What is the difference between pipelined and SAR ADCs?
faqs.answer: Pipelined ADCs and SAR ADCs have different architectures suited for different applications: (1) Pipelined ADCs - use multiple stages operating in parallel to achieve high sampling rates (100MSPS to GSPS), ideal for communications and wideband applications; (2) SAR ADCs - use successive approximation for high resolution (16-24 bits) at moderate speeds (up to 10MSPS), ideal for precision measurement and industrial applications. Rorebai offers both types: CBM94xxx series pipelined ADCs for high-speed applications and CBM92xxx series SAR ADCs for precision applications. Choose pipelined for speed, SAR for resolution.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose pipelined ADCs for high-speed applications; choose SAR ADCs for high-resolution precision measurement.

faqs.question: How do I evaluate ADC dynamic performance?
faqs.answer: ADC dynamic performance is characterized by several key parameters: (1) SNR (Signal-to-Noise Ratio) - measures signal quality relative to noise floor, higher is better; (2) SFDR (Spurious-Free Dynamic Range) - measures distortion products relative to fundamental, higher is better; (3) ENOB (Effective Number of Bits) - actual resolution considering noise and distortion; (4) THD (Total Harmonic Distortion) - measures harmonic distortion; (5) SINAD (Signal-to-Noise and Distortion) - combines SNR and THD. For communications, SFDR > 70dBc and SNR > 65dBFS are typically required. Rorebai ADCs provide detailed dynamic performance specifications in their datasheets.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate ADCs based on SNR, SFDR, and ENOB for your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: What input drive requirements do Rorebai ADCs have?
faqs.answer: Rorebai high-speed ADCs have specific input drive requirements: (1) Input impedance - typically 50Ω differential or 1kΩ single-ended; (2) Input range - varies by model, commonly 1Vpp to 2Vpp differential; (3) Bandwidth - driver amplifier should have bandwidth > 2x ADC sampling rate; (4) Noise - driver noise should be well below ADC noise floor; (5) Distortion - driver THD should be better than ADC specification. Rorebai provides recommended driver amplifiers and reference circuits in their datasheets. For best performance, use differential signaling and proper termination.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use recommended driver amplifiers with adequate bandwidth and low noise for optimal ADC performance.

faqs.question: How do I interface with Rorebai ADCs?
faqs.answer: Rorebai ADCs support multiple digital interfaces: (1) LVDS - low-voltage differential signaling for high-speed data transfer, most common for high-speed ADCs; (2) CMOS - single-ended interface for lower speed applications; (3) JESD204B/C - high-speed serial interface for very high sampling rates; (4) SPI - for configuration and control registers. Interface selection depends on sampling rate and system requirements. Rorebai ADCs typically use LVDS for data output and SPI for configuration. Reference designs and FPGA interface examples are available from LiTong.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use LVDS for high-speed data, SPI for configuration; JESD204 for very high sampling rates.

faqs.question: What clocking requirements do high-speed ADCs have?
faqs.answer: High-speed ADC clocking requirements: (1) Clock jitter - sampling clock jitter directly impacts SNR, use low-jitter clock sources (<1ps RMS); (2) Clock frequency - must match ADC sampling rate requirements; (3) Clock amplitude - typically LVDS or LVPECL levels; (4) Duty cycle - 50% duty cycle required for optimal performance; (5) Clock distribution - use proper clock buffers for multiple ADCs. Rorebai ADCs provide clock input specifications in their datasheets. For multi-channel systems, use clock distribution chips to ensure phase alignment. Contact LiTong FAE for clocking design support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use low-jitter clock sources and proper clock distribution for optimal ADC performance.

products.name: 14-Bit 250MSPS High-Speed ADC
products.shortDescription: High-performance 14-bit ADC with 250MSPS sampling rate, 72dB SNR, and LVDS output for communications and instrumentat...
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM94AD67 is a high-performance 14-bit analog-to-digital converter designed for demanding communications and instrumentation applications. It features a pipelined architecture achieving 250MSPS sampling rate with excellent dynamic performance.

With 72dB SNR and 85dBc SFDR, the CBM94AD67 delivers high-fidelity signal conversion for wideband receivers and transmitters. The differential input accepts 1.5Vpp signals with flexible common-mode voltage.

The ADC provides LVDS digital outputs and SPI configuration interface. Operating from 1.8V and 3.3V supplies, it consumes only 380mW typical power. The device is available in a compact 7x7mm QFN package.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High-Speed Data Conversion
faeReview.content: The CBM94AD67 is an excellent ADC that I've successfully deployed in multiple communication systems. In my experience, the 72dB SNR and 85dBc SFDR are competitive with international brands at a much better price point. I particularly appreciate the flexible input configuration which simplifies front-end design. The LVDS outputs integrate well with FPGA-based systems. One key recommendation: pay close attention to clock jitter as it directly impacts SNR performance. With a clean clock source, customers can achieve the full 72dB SNR specification. The 380mW power consumption is reasonable for this performance class. For cost-sensitive communication designs, this ADC offers excellent value.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance ADC ideal for communications with excellent price-performance ratio

alternativeParts.reason: Established brand with extensive ecosystem support

alternativeParts.useCase: Power-sensitive applications with lower speed requirements

companionParts.description: Low-noise amplifier for ADC input drive
Low-jitter clock generator for ADC sampling

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of CBM94AD67?
faqs.answer: The CBM94AD67 typical power consumption is 380mW at 250MSPS sampling rate with all digital outputs active. Power consumption scales with sampling rate: approximately 300mW at 125MSPS and 380mW at 250MSPS. The device supports power-down modes reducing consumption to <10mW when disabled. For power-sensitive applications, consider disabling unused digital outputs and using lower sampling rates when possible. The 1.8V digital supply can be reduced to save additional power in some applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 380mW typical at full speed; use power-down modes for battery applications.

faqs.question: What is the input bandwidth of CBM94AD67?
faqs.answer: The CBM94AD67 features a wide analog input bandwidth of 650MHz (-3dB), enabling direct RF sampling applications. The full-power bandwidth supports input frequencies up to 300MHz while maintaining rated SNR performance. For undersampling applications, the device can sample signals well above Nyquist. The differential input structure provides excellent common-mode rejection. Input impedance is approximately 4kΩ differential in parallel with 2pF. For wideband applications, use appropriate anti-aliasing filters and termination.
faqs.decisionGuide: 650MHz bandwidth supports direct RF sampling; use anti-aliasing filters for best performance.

faqs.question: How do I configure CBM94AD67 registers?
faqs.answer: The CBM94AD67 is configured through a 3-wire SPI interface: (1) CS - chip select, active low; (2) SCLK - serial clock, up to 20MHz; (3) SDIO - serial data input/output. Configuration steps: (1) Assert CS low; (2) Send 16-bit command (7-bit address + 9-bit data); (3) Deassert CS high. Key registers include: 0x00 - chip configuration, 0x01 - clock divider, 0x02 - output mode, 0x03 - test patterns. The device powers up with default settings suitable for most applications. Detailed register map is provided in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SPI interface for configuration; default settings work for most applications.

faqs.question: What output formats does CBM94AD67 support?
faqs.answer: The CBM94AD67 supports multiple output formats: (1) Offset binary - unipolar coding from 0 to 16383; (2) Two's complement - bipolar coding from -8192 to +8191; (3) Gray code - for special applications. The default is offset binary. Output mode is configured via SPI register 0x02. The LVDS outputs operate at 250MSPS x 14 bits = 3.5Gbps total data rate. The device supports double-data-rate (DDR) LVDS to reduce pin count. Output timing is adjustable via register settings to align with FPGA capture.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use offset binary for unipolar, two's complement for bipolar applications.

faqs.question: Does CBM94AD67 support multi-chip synchronization?
faqs.answer: Yes, the CBM94AD67 supports multi-chip synchronization for phased array and MIMO applications: (1) SYNC input - external synchronization signal aligns internal clocks; (2) SYSREF input - JESD204B-style reference for deterministic latency; (3) Clock distribution - use same clock source for all ADCs; (4) Digital reset - synchronous reset ensures aligned data output. For precise phase matching, use matched PCB traces for clock and analog inputs. The device-to-device phase mismatch is typically <100ps. Contact LiTong FAE for multi-chip synchronization design support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SYNC and SYSREF inputs for multi-chip synchronization in array applications.

products.name: 16-Bit 1MSPS Precision ADC
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 16-bit SAR ADC with 1MSPS sampling rate, 91dB SNR, and SPI interface for industrial and medical appli...
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM92AD16 is a high-resolution 16-bit successive approximation register (SAR) ADC designed for precision measurement applications. It achieves 1MSPS sampling rate with excellent DC and AC performance.

With 91dB SNR and ±1 LSB INL, the CBM92AD16 delivers accurate conversion for sensor interfaces and data acquisition systems. The pseudo-differential input accepts 0-VREF unipolar or ±VREF/2 bipolar signals.

The ADC provides SPI digital interface and operates from a single 5V supply, consuming only 15mW typical power. The device is available in a compact 4x4mm QFN package suitable for space-constrained designs.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Precision Analog
faeReview.content: The CBM92AD16 is a solid choice for precision measurement applications. In my experience with industrial customers, the ±1 LSB INL and 91dB SNR are impressive for this price point. The flexibility of unipolar/bipolar input is valuable for sensor interface designs. I particularly like the single 5V supply operation which simplifies power supply design compared to dual-supply alternatives. The 15mW power consumption enables battery-powered data acquisition. One tip: use a clean reference voltage as ADC performance is directly dependent on reference quality. With proper PCB layout and a good reference, this ADC delivers consistent, accurate results.
faeReview.highlight: High-resolution ADC ideal for precision industrial and medical applications

companionParts.description: Precision voltage reference for ADC reference input
Precision amplifier for sensor signal conditioning

faqs.question: What is the price difference between CBM92AD16 and competitors?
faqs.answer: The CBM92AD16 is positioned as a cost-effective solution with approximately 30-40% lower price compared to established competitors like ADI and TI. The exact pricing depends on volume and configuration. Despite the lower price, the CBM92AD16 delivers comparable performance with 91dB SNR and ±1 LSB INL. This makes it an excellent choice for cost-sensitive industrial and medical applications without sacrificing performance. Contact LiTong sales for specific pricing based on your volume requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: CBM92AD16 offers excellent value for high-volume precision measurement applications.

faqs.question: Can CBM92AD16 be used in battery-powered applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the CBM92AD16 is well-suited for battery-powered applications with its low 15mW power consumption. At 1MSPS sampling rate, it consumes only 15mW typical. The device also features power-down modes reducing consumption to <1μA when disabled. For continuous sampling, a typical 1000mAh battery could power the ADC for approximately 2700 hours (112 days). The single 5V supply operation simplifies power supply design for battery systems. Contact LiTong FAE for power optimization recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low 15mW power consumption makes CBM92AD16 suitable for battery-powered data acquisition.

products.name: 14-Bit 250MSPS High-Speed DAC
products.shortDescription: High-performance 14-bit DAC with 250MSPS update rate, 80dB SFDR, and LVDS input for communications and waveform generation.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM94DA67 is a high-performance 14-bit digital-to-analog converter designed for communications and waveform generation applications. It features a current-steering architecture achieving 250MSPS update rate with excellent dynamic performance.

With 80dB SFDR and 72dB SNR, the CBM94DA67 delivers high-fidelity signal reconstruction for transmitters and arbitrary waveform generators. The differential current output provides flexible interfacing to various loads.

The DAC accepts LVDS digital inputs and provides differential current output. Operating from 1.8V and 3.3V supplies, it consumes only 350mW typical power. The device is available in a compact 7x7mm QFN package.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - High-Speed Data Conversion
faeReview.content: The CBM94DA67 is an excellent DAC that pairs perfectly with the CBM94AD67 ADC for complete transceiver designs. The 80dB SFDR is impressive for this performance class and enables clean signal generation for communications. I particularly appreciate the differential current output which provides flexibility in interfacing to various loads and filters. The LVDS input integrates well with FPGA-based systems. For best SFDR performance, ensure clean power supplies and proper clocking. The 350mW power consumption is competitive. For cost-sensitive communication designs requiring high-speed DACs, this device offers excellent value.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance DAC ideal for communications transmitters

faqs.question: What is the output format of CBM94DA67?
faqs.answer: The CBM94DA67 provides differential current output: (1) Output current - 20mA full-scale nominal; (2) Compliance voltage - up to 1.25V; (3) Differential output - IOUTP and IOUTN pins; (4) Load options - resistive load to ground or center-tapped transformer; (5) Reconstruction filter - external filter required for smoothing; (6) Output impedance - high impedance current source. The current output provides flexibility in interfacing to various loads and enables excellent dynamic performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use resistive load or transformer coupling based on application requirements.

products.name: Precision Low-Noise Operational Amplifier
products.shortDescription: High-precision op-amp with 10MHz bandwidth, 2.5nV/√Hz noise, and 50μV offset for sensor signal conditioning.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMOPA267 is a high-precision operational amplifier designed for sensor signal conditioning and precision measurement applications. It features excellent DC precision with low offset voltage and drift combined with low noise performance.

With 50μV maximum offset voltage, 2.5nV/√Hz input noise density, and 10MHz gain bandwidth product, the CBMOPA267 delivers precision performance for demanding applications. The rail-to-rail output maximizes dynamic range.
The op-amp operates from single 2.7V to 5.5V supplies and consumes only 2.5mA typical supply current. The device is available in SOT23-5 and SOIC-8 packages.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE - Precision Analog
faeReview.content: The CBMOPA267 is a versatile precision op-amp that I've used in numerous sensor conditioning designs. The 50μV offset is excellent for this class of amplifier and the 2.5nV/√Hz noise enables high-resolution signal conditioning. I particularly like the combination of precision DC performance with 10MHz bandwidth - many precision amps sacrifice bandwidth. The rail-to-rail output is valuable for maximizing dynamic range in single-supply designs. For best performance, pay attention to layout - keep traces short and use proper grounding. This op-amp is pin-compatible with popular industry standards making substitution easy.
faeReview.highlight: Precision op-amp ideal for sensor signal conditioning

faqs.question: What is the noise performance of CBMOPA267?
faqs.answer: CBMOPA267 noise characteristics: (1) Voltage noise - 2.5nV/√Hz at 1kHz; (2) Current noise - 1.5pA/√Hz; (3) 0.1-10Hz noise - 0.5μVp-p; (4) Noise figure - depends on source impedance; (5) Bandwidth - 10MHz GBW; (6) Corner frequency - ~100Hz. The low noise makes this op-amp suitable for high-resolution sensor conditioning. For lowest noise, keep source impedance low and minimize external resistor values.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent noise performance for high-resolution signal conditioning.

categories.name: High-Speed DACs
categories.description: Rorebai high-speed DACs provide high-fidelity digital-to-analog conversion for communications, instrumentation, and waveform generation applications. The portfolio includes current-steering architectures with update rates from 100MSPS to over 1GSPS, resolutions from 12-bit to 16-bit.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Rorebai High-Speed DACs
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right DAC for your signal generation application

faqs.question: What is the difference between voltage and current output DACs?
faqs.answer: Voltage output DACs provide a voltage signal directly, while current output DACs provide a current that must be converted to voltage using a resistor or transimpedance amplifier. Current output DACs generally offer better high-frequency performance and are preferred for high-speed applications. Voltage output DACs are simpler to use but may have limited bandwidth. Rorebai high-speed DACs use current-steering architecture for optimal dynamic performance.

products.name: 14-Bit 250MSPS High-Speed DAC
products.shortDescription: High-performance 14-bit DAC with 250MSPS update rate, 80dB SFDR, and LVDS input for communications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM94DA67 is a high-performance 14-bit digital-to-analog converter designed for communications and waveform generation applications.
With 80dB SFDR and 72dB SNR, the CBM94DA67 delivers high-fidelity signal reconstruction for transmitters and arbitrary waveform generators.
The DAC accepts LVDS digital inputs and provides differential current output. Operating from 1.8V and 3.3V supplies, it consumes only 350mW typical power.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: The CBM94DA67 is an excellent DAC that pairs perfectly with the CBM94AD67 ADC for complete transceiver designs. The 80dB SFDR is impressive for this performance class.
faeReview.highlight: High-performance DAC ideal for communications transmitters

products.name: 14-Bit 125MSPS Low-Power DAC
products.shortDescription: Low-power 14-bit DAC with 125MSPS update rate, optimized for portable and battery-powered applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM94DA14 is a low-power 14-bit DAC designed for portable communications and battery-powered applications. It features 125MSPS update rate with excellent dynamic performance.
With 75dB SFDR and 68dB SNR, the CBM94DA14 delivers good signal quality while consuming only 180mW typical power.
The DAC accepts CMOS/LVDS digital inputs and provides single-ended or differential current output. Operating from 1.8V and 3.3V supplies.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The CBM94DA14 offers an excellent balance of performance and power consumption. Ideal for battery-powered applications where every milliwatt counts.
faeReview.highlight: Low-power DAC ideal for portable applications

products.name: 16-Bit 1GSPS Ultra-High-Speed DAC
products.shortDescription: Ultra-high-performance 16-bit DAC with 1GSPS update rate for wideband communications and radar applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM96DA16 is an ultra-high-performance 16-bit DAC designed for wideband communications, radar, and electronic warfare applications. It features 1GSPS update rate with exceptional dynamic performance.
With 82dB SFDR and 75dB SNR at 1GSPS, the CBM96DA16 delivers industry-leading performance for demanding wideband signal generation.
The DAC accepts JESD204B digital inputs and provides differential current output. Operating from 1.2V, 1.8V, and 3.3V supplies.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: The CBM96DA16 delivers exceptional performance for wideband applications. The 1GSPS rate and 16-bit resolution enable generation of complex wideband signals.

products.name: 12-Bit 500MSPS High-Speed DAC
products.shortDescription: High-speed 12-bit DAC with 500MSPS update rate for cost-sensitive communications applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBM92DA12 is a high-speed 12-bit DAC designed for cost-sensitive communications and instrumentation applications. It features 500MSPS update rate with good dynamic performance.
With 70dB SFDR and 65dB SNR, the CBM92DA12 delivers adequate signal quality for many communications applications at an attractive price point.
The DAC accepts CMOS digital inputs and provides differential current output. Operating from 3.3V supply.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The CBM92DA12 offers excellent value for cost-sensitive applications. The 500MSPS rate and good SFDR make it suitable for many communications designs.

categories.name: RF Transceivers
categories.description: Rorebai RF transceivers provide integrated RF solutions for wireless communications. The portfolio includes wideband PLLs, transmitters, and receivers supporting multiple frequency bands for cellular, ISM, and satellite applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Rorebai RF Transceivers
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right RF transceiver for your wireless application

faqs.question: What frequency bands do Rorebai RF transceivers support?
faqs.answer: Rorebai RF transceivers support a wide range of frequency bands. The CBMRF900 series covers 600MHz to 6GHz for cellular applications including 4G LTE and 5G NR. The CBMRF2400 series is optimized for 2.4GHz ISM band applications including WiFi, Bluetooth, and proprietary protocols.

products.name: Wideband RF Transceiver for 4G/5G
products.shortDescription: High-performance wideband RF transceiver supporting 600MHz to 6GHz for 4G/5G base stations and small cells.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMRF9009 is a high-performance wideband RF transceiver designed for 4G/5G base stations, small cells, and massive MIMO applications. It supports frequency ranges from 600MHz to 6GHz.
With integrated transmitter and receiver chains, the CBMRF9009 provides a complete RF solution. The device features excellent phase noise performance and low EVM for high-order modulation schemes.
The transceiver supports TDD and FDD operation modes with fast switching times. Digital interface enables integration with baseband processors.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - RF Systems
faeReview.content: The CBMRF9009 is a versatile transceiver that covers all major cellular bands. The integrated design simplifies RF frontend development for base stations.

products.name: 2.4GHz ISM Band Transceiver
products.shortDescription: Low-power 2.4GHz transceiver optimized for ISM band applications including IoT and wireless sensors.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMRF2400 is a low-power 2.4GHz transceiver designed for ISM band applications including IoT devices, wireless sensors, and smart home products.
With integrated PA, LNA, and frequency synthesizer, the CBMRF2400 provides a complete RF solution in a compact package. The device features low power consumption for battery-powered applications.
The transceiver supports multiple modulation schemes and data rates up to 2Mbps. SPI interface enables easy integration with microcontrollers.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMRF2400 is perfect for IoT applications. The low power consumption and compact size make it ideal for battery-powered wireless sensors.

products.name: 5.8GHz WLAN Transceiver
products.shortDescription: High-performance 5.8GHz transceiver for WLAN and high-data-rate wireless applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMRF5800 is a high-performance 5.8GHz transceiver designed for WLAN, WiFi, and high-data-rate wireless applications. It supports 802.11a/n/ac standards.
With integrated 802.11 MAC/baseband and RF frontend, the CBMRF5800 provides a complete WLAN solution. The device supports data rates up to 433Mbps.
The transceiver features advanced calibration and compensation circuits for robust operation across temperature and process variations.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMRF5800 provides a complete WLAN solution with excellent performance. The integrated design reduces BOM cost and simplifies implementation.

products.name: Sub-1GHz Long-Range Transceiver
products.shortDescription: Sub-1GHz transceiver optimized for long-range, low-power IoT and smart metering applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMRFSUB1G is a sub-1GHz transceiver designed for long-range, low-power IoT applications including smart metering, industrial monitoring, and agricultural sensors.
Operating in 433MHz, 868MHz, or 915MHz bands, the CBMRFSUB1G provides excellent range and penetration. The device features high sensitivity for reliable long-distance communication.
The transceiver supports multiple modulation schemes including FSK, GFSK, and OOK. Low power modes enable years of operation on coin cell batteries.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMRFSUB1G excels in long-range IoT applications. The sub-1GHz frequency provides excellent range and building penetration compared to 2.4GHz.

categories.name: Precision Operational Amplifiers
categories.description: Rorebai precision operational amplifiers provide low-offset, low-noise amplification for sensor conditioning, instrumentation, and precision measurement applications. The portfolio includes chopper-stabilized, low-noise, and high-speed precision op-amps.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Rorebai Precision Op-Amps
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to selecting the right op-amp for your precision application

faqs.question: What is the difference between standard and chopper-stabilized op-amps?
faqs.answer: Standard op-amps have input offset voltages typically in the millivolt range, while chopper-stabilized op-amps use auto-zeroing techniques to achieve microvolt-level offsets. Chopper-stabilized op-amps are preferred for high-precision DC measurements but may have higher noise at high frequencies.

products.name: Precision Low-Noise Operational Amplifier
products.shortDescription: High-precision op-amp with 10MHz bandwidth, 2.5nV/√Hz noise, and 50μV offset.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMOPA267 is a high-precision operational amplifier designed for sensor signal conditioning and precision measurement applications.
With 50μV maximum offset voltage, 2.5nV/√Hz input noise density, and 10MHz gain bandwidth product, the CBMOPA267 delivers precision performance.
The op-amp operates from single 2.7V to 5.5V supplies and consumes only 2.5mA typical supply current.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMOPA267 is a versatile precision op-amp. The combination of low offset, low noise, and good bandwidth makes it suitable for many precision applications.
faeReview.highlight: Precision op-amp ideal for sensor conditioning

products.name: Zero-Drift Chopper-Stabilized Op-Amp
products.shortDescription: Ultra-precision chopper-stabilized op-amp with 1μV offset and zero drift for high-precision DC measurements.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMOPAZ201 is an ultra-precision chopper-stabilized operational amplifier designed for high-precision DC measurement applications.
With 1μV maximum input offset voltage and zero drift over temperature, the CBMOPAZ201 delivers exceptional DC precision for bridge sensors, thermocouples, and other low-level signals.
The op-amp operates from single 2.2V to 5.5V supplies with rail-to-rail input and output.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMOPAZ201 delivers exceptional DC precision. The 1μV offset and zero drift make it perfect for bridge sensors and precision measurements.

products.name: High-Speed Precision Op-Amp
products.shortDescription: High-speed precision op-amp with 100MHz bandwidth and 200V/μs slew rate for fast signal conditioning.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMOPA516 is a high-speed precision operational amplifier designed for fast signal conditioning and high-speed data acquisition applications.
With 100MHz gain bandwidth product, 200V/μs slew rate, and 500μV offset voltage, the CBMOPA516 combines speed and precision.
The op-amp operates from ±5V to ±15V dual supplies or 10V to 30V single supply.

faeReview.author: David Liu
faeReview.title: Principal FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMOPA516 offers an excellent combination of speed and precision. The 100MHz bandwidth and low offset make it ideal for driving high-speed ADCs.
faeReview.highlight: High-speed precision op-amp for fast signals

products.name: Ultra-Low-Power Precision Op-Amp
products.shortDescription: Ultra-low-power precision op-amp with 20μA supply current for battery-powered sensor applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The CBMOPA802 is an ultra-low-power precision operational amplifier designed for battery-powered and energy-harvesting sensor applications.
With only 20μA supply current per channel, 100μV offset voltage, and 350kHz bandwidth, the CBMOPA802 delivers precision performance with minimal power consumption.
The op-amp operates from 1.8V to 5.5V single supply with rail-to-rail input and output.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: The CBMOPA802 is perfect for battery-powered applications. The 20μA current consumption enables years of operation on coin cell batteries.
